name: 1300 MH CALL
description: 1300 MH CALL is our confidential mental health triage service providing advice and information to Queenslanders experiencing a mental health emergency or crisis. If needed, you can be triaged directly to a Queensland public mental health service or acute care team.

name: 13YARN
description: 13YARN provides confidential crisis support for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people living anywhere in Australia. This is a no shame, no judgement, safe place to yarn with experienced Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander workers.
eligibility: Aboriginal people and Torres Strait Islander people

name: 1800ELDERHelp
description: 1800 ELDERHelp (1800 353 374) is a free call phone number that automatically redirects callers seeking information and advice on elder abuse with the phone service in their state or territory. Elder abuse phone lines are not crisis support services, and operating hours and services vary across states and territories.

name: 1800RESPECT
description: 1800RESPECT is the national 24/7 sexual assault, domestic and family violence counselling service for people experiencing, or at risk of experiencing violence, their friends and family, colleagues and employers.

name: 54 Reasons SHIELD program
description: This service can help you apply for financial assistance through Victim Assist. 54 Reasons provides trauma-informed support for young and adult males in Queensland who are victims of sexual violence.

name: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Family Wellbeing Services
description: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Family Wellbeing Services provide a free and confidential one stop shop for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander families across Queensland. They provide support to improve your social, emotional, physical and spiritual wellbeing, and help you be able to safely care for and protect your children.
eligibility: Aboriginal people and Torres Strait Islander people

name: Acquired Brain Injury Outreach Service (ABIOS)
description: A local support service for people with an acquired brain injury living in the Greater Brisbane area.

name: Act for Kids
description: A national service providing therapy and support services to children and families who have experienced, or are at risk of harm.

name: Anglicare North Queensland
description: Anglicare NQ provides a wide range of local services to support young people across North Queensland area. Services include youth support programs, case management, outreach, emergency accommodation, and non-family-based residential care.

name: Beyond Blue
description: Beyond Blue can provide support if you or a loved one need help with anxiety or depression.

name: Blue Knot Foundation
description: Blue Knot support adult survivors of childhood trauma and abuse. They also support their parents, partners, family and friends.

name: Bravehearts
description: Bravehearts provide support and counselling for people who have been hurt by childhood sexual abuse. They provide counselling services for children, families and adult survivors in Queensland.

name: Brisbane Rape and Incest Survivors Support Centre (BRISSC)
description: A local support service for women in the Greater Brisbane area who have experienced sexual assault at any time in their lives.

name: Brisbane Youth Service
description: A local support service for young people in Brisbane who are experiencing a broad range of difficulties. Services include mental health, family, relationships, parenting, housing and health supports.

name: Cairns Sexual Assault Service
description: This Cairns service provides inclusive support for people over 12 years, who have been impacted by sexual violence at any time in their life.

name: Caxton Legal Centre
description: Caxton provides legal and social welfare services to low income, homeless and vulnerable people in Brisbane.

name: Central Queensland Community Legal Centre
description: Central Queensland Community Legal Centre provides general legal advice, free of charge to people in Central and Northwest Queensland. They also hold legal advice clinics in Emerald, Gladstone, Mount Isa and Rockhampton throughout the week.

name: Centre Against Sexual Violence
description: Women in Logan and Redlands, who have experience sexual violence at any time, can get free confidential counselling at the Centre Against Sexual Violence. They also help young women over 12 years.

name: Community Legal Centres Queensland
description: Community Legal Centres Queensland can help you find a free, local community legal service that best suits your needs.

name: ConnectCare
description: ConnectCare provide case management and counselling support for individuals impacted by a non-violent property crime in Queensland.

name: DV Connect
description: 24/7 support for people who have been hurt or impacted by domestic and family violence.

name: Elder Abuse Prevention Unit
description: This helpline provides information, support and referrals for older Queenslanders who are experiencing abuse and people who witness the abuse of an older person.

name: Encircle Neighbourhood Centres
description: Encircle provides personal and family support for people in North Brisbane and the Moreton Bay region.

name: Family and Child Connect
description: If your family is going through tough times, contact Family and Child Connect for free, unlimited and confidential advice. They have local services across Queensland.

name: Gladstone Region Sexual Assault Centre
description: Help for Gladstone men, women and children who have experienced recent or historic sexual assaults.

name: Gold Coast Centre Against Sexual Violence
description: Help for Gold Coast women who have experienced sexual violence at any time in their lives.

name: Headspace
description: Headspace centres throughout Australia can help 12-25 year olds with mental health, physical health (including sexual health), alcohol and other drug or work and study issues.

name: Kalwun Development Corporation
description: Provides a broad range of social support service to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people living in Gold Coast and Logan area.
eligibility: Aboriginal people and Torres Strait Islander people

name: Kids Helpline
description: 24/7 help, support and information for young people aged 5 to 25.

name: Laurel Place
description: Help for all victims of sexual assault living in Sunshine Coast and Moreton Bay Regions.

name: Legal Aid Queensland
description: Legal information and advice for all Queenslanders.

name: Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Trans, Intersex Legal Service
description: Providing legal advice and information for people who identify as LGBTI experiencing related problems.

name: Lifeline
description: Lifeline provides compassionate support for people in crisis.

name: Mackay Sexual Health and Sexual Assault Service
description: Sexual assault services are available to men and women 16 years and over who have been raped, sexually assaulted or who are survivors of childhood sexual abuse.

name: Mununjali Housing and Development Company
description: Help for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples experiencing a broad range of difficulties.
eligibility: Aboriginal people and Torres Strait Islander people

name: Murrigunyah Family and Cultural Healing Centre
description: Support counselling for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander women and their families who are survivors or victims of sexual violence.
eligibility: Aboriginal people and Torres Strait Islander people

name: National Debt Helpline
description: National Debt Helpline is a not-for-profit service that helps people tackle their debt problems. Get free, independent and confidential financial advice.

name: National Disability Abuse and Neglect Hotline
description: A free, independent and confidential service for reporting abuse and neglect of people with disability.

name: National Redress Scheme
description: Specialist Redress Support Services are available for First Nations peoples, people from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, people with disability, young people, women, men, care leavers and former child migrants. They can help you understand the scheme and help you make an application.

name: North West HHS Sexual Health Services
description: Help with a broad range of sexual health concerns provided through Mount Isa Hospital.

name: Northern Peninsula Area Community Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Corporation
description: Provides a broad range of social support service to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people living in Queensland North Peninsula area.
eligibility: Aboriginal people and Torres Strait Islander people

name: Open Doors Youth Service
description: Support for lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender and intersex (LGBTI) young people.

name: Parentline
description: Call Parentline for free counselling and support for parents & carers in Queensland.

name: Phoenix House
description: Phoenix House offers free counselling and therapeutic supports to children, young people, and their families or caregivers in Bundaberg who have been affected by violence or abuse.

name: PACT
description: This service can help you apply for financial assistance through Victim Assist. Support for young victims and witnesses of crime who need to go to court in Queensland.

name: Queensland Advocacy Incorporated (QAI)
description: QAI provide free legal help and provide specialist support for victims with a disability.

name: Queensland Health - Public Hospitals and Health Care Facilities
description: Your local Queensland Health hospital or health care facility can support any victim in a broad range of ways. Emergency departments can provide 24/7 treatment for serious injuries and crisis care for people who have been sexually assaulted. Search for a Queensland Health service near you.

name: Queensland Health Victim Support Service
description: Supporting victims and families who have been hurt by someone experiencing mental illness or found to be unfit for trial.

name: Queensland Homicide Victims' Support Group (QHVSG)
description: This service can help you apply for financial assistance through Victim Assist. Help for family and friends of people who have died in a homicide.

name: Queensland Indigenous Family Violence Legal Service (QIFVLS)
description: Legal help for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples who are victims of family violence and /or sexual assault.
eligibility: Aboriginal people and Torres Strait Islander people

name: Queensland Sexual Assault Network (QSAN)
description: QSAN is a network of specialist sexual assault support services. Visit the QSAN website to search for a service in your local area. They also have a range of high quality self help resources for victims.

name: South Burnett CTC Youth and Family Services
description: South Burnett CTC Youth and Family Services supports people experiencing homelessness, domestic violence, health, parenting, education, employment and community issues.

name: Statewide Sexual Assault Helpline
description: Help, support and medical assistance for people who have been sexually assaulted.

name: Suicide Call Back Service
description: 24/7 telephone support and counselling for anyone effected by suicide.

name: Survivors & Mates Support Network
description: Survivors and mates can help men recover from sexual violence. Peer support is available by appointment. There website also has many high quality self help resources.

name: Tablelands Sexual Assault Service
description: Help and support for people in the Tablelands and surrounding areas who have experienced recent or historic sexual assault.

name: The Women's Centre
description: A women's services hub run by women for women, incorporating the Sexual Assault Support service, a Specialist Homelessness Service and a Women's Health Service.

name: True Support Services Hub
description: The True Support Services Hub (TSSH) provides acute sexual assault responses in Rockhampton. They can assist you with medical care, counselling, and support services. For acute medical assistance, go to the Rockhampton Emergency Department and ask for the True Support Service. For other support, phone True during business hours.

name: Universities Australia Student Safety Services
description: Access counselling and support for reporting sexual harassment and sexual assault at university.

name: Victim Assist Queensland
description: Victim Assist Queensland help victims of crime to get back to normal after experiencing violent crime or domestic and family violence in Queensland.

name: VictimConnect
description: This service can help you apply for financial assistance through Victim Assist. VictimConnect provides 24/7 support for all victims of violence that happens in Queensland. They can help you access financial assistance, counselling and case management and court support.

name: Victims Registers
description: Victims Registers help victims stay informed about the movements of a person who has been convicted of a crime committed against them. If you have been a victim of violence committed in Queensland, you can apply to go on a Victims Register.

name: Wide Bay Women's Health Centre
description: Help for women, young people and children in Wide Bay who have experienced sexual assault.

name: Women's Legal Service Queensland
description: Legal help and social work support for women working through issues related to family law, child support, domestic and family violence or child protection.

name: WWILD
description: This service can help you apply for financial assistance through Victim Assist. WWILD supports for people with intellectual disabilities who are survivors of crime. They can help you access financial assistance and other supports.

name: Youth Advocacy Centre (YAC)
description: YAC offers free legal services, youth support and family support assistance and services to young people in Greater Brisbane

name: Youth and Family Services (YFS)
description: YFS helps young people experiencing a broad range of difficulties, including family violence and legal issues.

name: Zig Zag Young Women's Resource Centre
description: Zig Zag supports young women who have experienced sexual violence or family violence. They provide counselling, group programs and other practical support.
